_note: Baselined dependency advisories for the CI dependency-audit gate (issue #22). Keyed by GHSA ID. The gate fails only on critical/high advisories NOT listed here. To ACCEPT a new finding: add it with a reason + accepted_on date under security-engineer review. To mark RESOLVED: remove its entry once the dependency is bumped. Optional review_spec (the tracking spec that will resolve it) and review_by (ISO date) mark a time-boxed acceptance: past review_by the gate warns on every run and FAILS the nightly scheduled re-audit, so a slipped fix goes loud instead of silent. A review_spec with review_by:null is flagged pending until the tracking spec has a target date.

GHSA-5xrq-8626-4rwp.title: When Vitest UI server is listening, arbitrary file can be read and executed
GHSA-5xrq-8626-4rwp.reason: Vitest UI server arbitrary file read/exec. Dev/test tooling only, never shipped to patients or run in prod. Accepted pending the vitest 2->3 bump (spec 30, resolved vitest 3.2.6).

GHSA-36qx-fr4f-26g5.title: Next.js has a Middleware / Proxy bypass in Pages Router applications using i18n
GHSA-36qx-fr4f-26g5.reason: Next.js middleware/proxy bypass (Pages Router i18n). Production framework, patient-facing runtime; only a breaking 14->15 major closes it. Tracked in spec #29 (#16 track 2).

GHSA-8h8q-6873-q5fj.title: Next.js Vulnerable to Denial of Service with Server Components
GHSA-8h8q-6873-q5fj.reason: Next.js DoS via Server Components. Production framework, patient-facing runtime; only a breaking 14->15 major closes it. Tracked in spec #29 (#16 track 2).

GHSA-c4j6-fc7j-m34r.title: Next.js vulnerable to server-side request forgery in applications using WebSocket upgrades
GHSA-c4j6-fc7j-m34r.reason: Next.js SSRF via WebSocket upgrades (CVSS 8.6). Production framework, patient-facing runtime, reachable on the self-hosted deployment; only a breaking 14->15 major closes it. Tracked in spec #29 (#16 track 2).

GHSA-fx2h-pf6j-xcff.title: vite: `server.fs.deny` bypass on Windows alternate paths
GHSA-fx2h-pf6j-xcff.reason: Vite server.fs.deny bypass (Windows dev server). Dev/build tooling only, not shipped. Accepted pending the resolved vite reaching >=6.4.3 (spec 30 Path 2: overrides.vite ^6.4.3, vitest kept at 3.2.6).

GHSA-h25m-26qc-wcjf.title: Next.js HTTP request deserialization can lead to DoS when using insecure React Server Components
GHSA-h25m-26qc-wcjf.reason: Next.js HTTP request deserialization DoS. Production framework, patient-facing runtime; only a breaking 14->15 major closes it. Tracked in spec #29 (#16 track 2).

GHSA-q4gf-8mx6-v5v3.title: Next.js has a Denial of Service with Server Components
GHSA-q4gf-8mx6-v5v3.reason: Next.js DoS with Server Components. Production framework, patient-facing runtime; only a breaking 14->15 major closes it. Tracked in spec #29 (#16 track 2).
